preconditions: Odin and Rust toolchains are installed
assumptions: The x86_64 data model is LP64
The heartbeat payload uses natural C layout without packed annotations

steps.title: Add the normative heartbeat payload struct and project it into Odin and Rust bindings
Wire the typed heartbeat payload through the Odin kernel and Rust service scaffold
Add full layout parity validation and regenerate control artifacts

expected_outputs: The ABI defines a normative heartbeat payload with a 24-byte C layout
Generated Odin and Rust bindings expose layout-compatible heartbeat payload structs
The kernel and service both use the typed payload path
The harness verifies total layout parity across the projections

invariants: The heartbeat payload layout is derived from the normative C contract
The Odin and Rust projections preserve identical size, alignment, and field offsets
expected_behavior: The generator emits a layout-compatible heartbeat payload struct into both bindings
odin check and cargo check pass with the typed payload path in use
verify.sh reports a layout_parity check with a passing structural_integrity subsystem
actual_behavior: The generator emits a 24-byte heartbeat payload struct into both Odin and Rust bindings.
The Odin kernel populates timestamp and status_bits through the typed payload path and logs the payload values over serial.
The Rust service initializes and mutates the typed HeartbeatPayload before issuing the ABI-backed heartbeat request.
verify.sh reports a passing layout_parity check with the structural_integrity subsystem.

explanation_summary: Added the normative heartbeat payload struct to the ABI and projected it into Odin and Rust with natural C layout.
Updated the kernel and service layers to use the typed heartbeat payload path with ABI-backed handles and syscalls.
Added a layout parity validator that proves size, alignment, and field offsets match across the C contract and both projections.
